title: vatica micrantha
intro: Vatica micrantha, also known as the small-leafed vatica or tembaga, is a species of plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is primarily found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The tembaga leaves can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are also used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
